methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $177M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Carbon Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Paul Mikesell that has raised $177M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 same-sector peers at comparable or adjacent stages, comprising 8 private companies and 1 public equity (ticker 277810.KQ). Key private comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $301M valuation from the $177M capital raised proxy. Peer-median triangulation across the 9-company set yields a $1.6B implied valuation, with the broader peer range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in market positioning and capital efficiency across the cohort.

Blending both methods produces a triangulated range with a low of $256M, median of $950M, and high of $1.8B. The $694M spread between stage-progression and peer-median outputs reflects material uncertainty around market multiples for Series C robotics companies. Key uncertainties include Carbon Robotics' relative technology differentiation, customer concentration, path to unit economics, and whether peer valuations reflect durable market fundamentals or froth in frontier-tech equity.
